title: Nallavana Kettavana Lyrics Savaale Samaali Song Lyrics
stanzas:
Nallavana Kettavana Lyrics Savaale Samaali
Singer(s): L. R. Eswari, Anthony Daasan
Music:  Thaman S

ada aambalayil
uthamana kaanala.
ini boomiyile porappanu thonala.
ada evanukkumey
ponna madhikka theriyala.
ada engamela enna kovam
puriyala.

Ponnungala eppodhumey
bodha porulaa nenaikkuraan.
maaney thaeney mayilenuthaan
poiyaa solli kavukkuraan.

Kadhalyum kaamthayum
onnaa thaaney nenaikkuraan.
paavamnu irangiponaa
pattaa pottu kudhikkuraan.

ponnungale iva eppodhumey
dhinam kattilukku mattumthaaney
laayakkunu nenaikkuraan.

Kaariandhaan ada mudinjichinaa
ada adutha ponna thaedi
avan naaya pola alayuraan.

ava kannu rendum
nalla paamba kadavule.
ava paarthaa vesam
aeruthada kadavule.

Ava sirippu kooda
thookku kayiru kadavule.
adhula maattikkitu
thonguromey kadavule.

aambalanga aattathathaan
ponna vachi mudikkira nee.
indha ponnungala
padachathaala
maatikittu thavikkiraney.

Aambalaikku eppodhumey
pombalathaan sagunidaa.
eppo enna seivaalonu
uthth neeyum kavanidaa.

Kaasu panam irundhuchinna
kaala kooda pudikkuraa
kadhalunnu solli solli
pocketayum karaikkuraa

MUllaa kuththum
ponnungale ada poovunu
sonna kavignargala
ada thookki pottu midhingadaa.

adangaadha ponnungala
ada thappaa valarththa
appaakkala - ammaakkala
katti vachchi adingadaa.

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

Nallavana Kettavana conveys themes of joy and celebration, highlighting the playful dynamics between characters.

What is the context of this song in the movie?

In Savaale Samaali, this song is performed during a lively celebration, showcasing the characters' happiness and camaraderie.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song features the talented voices of Anthonydasan and L R Eswari.

When was the song released?

Nallavana Kettavana was released on March 28, 2015.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Nallavana Kettavana, the lively duet between Anthonydasan and L R Eswari encapsulates the spirit of celebration and joy. The song is set against a backdrop of festivities in Savaale Samaali, where characters express their happiness and playful banter. Its energetic beats and catchy lyrics evoke a sense of excitement, making it a perfect anthem for parties and celebrations. The song's vibrant mood resonates with audiences, inviting them to join in the fun.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Nallavana Kettavana from Savaale Samaali is a vibrant celebration of life and joy, featuring the dynamic voices of Anthonydasan and L R Eswari. This upbeat track captures the essence of festivity, making it a perfect addition to any celebration. Experience the infectious energy and catchy rhythms that will keep you dancing all night long!
stanzaMeanings: Oh, the good and bad men are not known to the women.
In that woman, the best is seen. Now, it seems like there’s no place on earth for them. They don’t know how to treat anyone well. I don’t understand why they are angry with us.
All women are like gold, they are precious. The men who are kings don’t give them away.
Women always think of wealth and value. They lie saying they are like honey and the moon.
They think of love and desire as one. If they feel pity, they will just drink and forget.
These women always think of the bed every day.
If the matter is settled, they will search for the next woman like a dog.
All women are like gold, they are precious. The men who are kings don’t give them away.
Oh women, God has taken you away from us.
Her two eyes are like good snakes. When she looks, it feels like a trap.
Her smile is like a noose. We are trapped in it.
You are the one who ends the game with women. These women are suffering because of you.
Women are always like that, they are a blessing. Whenever I think of what to do, you should also remember.
If there is money, they will say love is there, and they will even empty their pockets.
Poets say that flowers are like thorns, they are lifted and placed on the head.
These women are never-ending, they are raised wrongly by fathers and mothers.
All women are like gold, they are precious. The men who are kings don’t give them away.
Oh, the good and bad men are not known to the women.
